title: API proposal: current refinements list + how to clear
body: I have a proposal to for listing current refinements and allowing to clear them. Here is a list of some use cases linked to clearing: 1. A clear button/link after a refinement list 2. A clear button/link before a refinement list 3. A clear button/link next to the title of the refinements list 4. A clear all button/link anywhere on the page 5. A list with all current refinements displaying like this: ``` js - Brand: Brand 1 (count) - Brand: Brand 2 (count) - Category: Cat 1 (count) - Category: Cat 2 (count) - Type: Type 1 (count) ``` 6. A list with all current refinements displaying like this: ``` Brand: | Brand 1 (count) X | Brand 2 (count) X Category: | Cat 1 (count) X | Cat 2 (count) X Type: | Type 1 (count) X ``` 7. 5 and 6 but with only a subset of these (For example without a numericRefinements facet) 8. 5, 6 and 7. but with different displays depending on the facet 9. 5, 6, 7 and 8. with the cross displayed before/not displayed 10. 5, 6, 7, 8. and 9. with a clear all button/link before 11. 5, 6, 7, 8 and 9. with a clear all button/link after And that's only what I could think of. So in the end we would go with: - `clearAll` : Link / button to clear all (See #359) - `currentRefinedValues`: List of refinements (optionally with a clear button added with CSS) (See #112) and a `clearAll` (See #359) Clearing specific facet refinements would probably need to go in the facet related widgets. Here are the API proposals (they're using #375): _clearAll_ (See #359) ``` js instantsearch.widgets.clearAll({ container: '#clear-all-link', cssClasses: {root, header, item, link, footer}, templates: { header: '', item: 'Clear all', // Called with {cssClasses, url} footer: '' }, hideContainerWhenNoRefinements: true }) ``` _currentRefinementList_ (See #112) ``` js search.addWidget( instantsearch.widgets.currentRefinedValues({ container: '#current-refinements-list', cssClasses: {root, header, body, clearAll, item, clearItem, footer}, facets: [ // Optional, if not specified, takes all of the facets { name: 'category'[, transformData: function (data) { /* ... */ return data; }]}, { name: 'brand'[, transformData: function (data) { /* ... */ return data; }]} ], templates: { header: '', item: '' + // Called with this.facets[current_facet.name].transformData({cssClasses, facet: {name[, label(||= facet.name)]}, name, count}) '', clearAll: '' + '' + ' Clear all' + '', footer: '' }, clearAll: 'after', // [true, false, 'before', 'after'] (true == 'before') hideContainerWhenNoRefinements: true }) ); ``` I chose to ask the user to explicitly specify which facets he wants to consider through the `facets` param for multiple reasons: - Being able to order the refinements - Giving him the ability to give us the same transformData than the one used in refinementList/menu/... - Possibility to set clear differently between facets - Give the ability to pass extra info specific to the facet (`label`) If we end up agreeing on this, I will create issues for each of them. What do you think?
